1929866035164795163: Note: Tariffs are paid by U.S. importers at the border and collected by the U.S. government; they aren’t paid by other countries. Tariff revenue goes to the U.S. Treasury and is a small share of federal receipts (about 1–2%; 1.6% FY2024). Importers and sometimes consumers bear the cost. See CBP stats and USAFacts data: https://www.cbp.gov/newsroom/stats/trade https://usafacts.org/answers/how-much-revenue-does-the-federal-government-collect-from-tariffs/country/united-states ([cbp.gov](https://www.cbp.gov/newsroom/stats/trade))
